updateCadence: Editorial slots can be updated daily from verified fixtures, team news, and AfroTools blog posts. Seed prompts are not user comments.

dailyMatchPreviews.label: Daily match preview
dailyMatchPreviews.title: How to read an African opener before lineups drop
dailyMatchPreviews.summary: Use travel rhythm, defensive shape, set pieces, and first-15-minute tempo as the preview frame while verified opponent and kickoff details are pending.

dailyMatchPreviews.label: Viewing guide
dailyMatchPreviews.title: Prime-time watch plan for diaspora rooms
dailyMatchPreviews.summary: Build a watch-party checklist around kickoff time conversion, WhatsApp invites, venue capacity, and prediction-card sharing.

africanTeamWatch.label: African team watch
africanTeamWatch.title: North Africa control teams
africanTeamWatch.summary: Watch compact mid-blocks, wide overloads, and dead-ball quality once verified fixtures arrive.

africanTeamWatch.label: African team watch
africanTeamWatch.title: West Africa transition teams
africanTeamWatch.summary: Track recovery speed, direct running, winger matchups, and how quickly each side turns clearances into pressure.

africanTeamWatch.label: African team watch
africanTeamWatch.title: Return and first-time storylines
africanTeamWatch.summary: Follow tournament emotion without overstating results: these rooms need context, travel notes, and fan explainers.

strategyPrompts.title: Press early or protect shape?
strategyPrompts.body: If your team scores first, should the coach keep pressing for the second goal or protect the midfield line?

strategyPrompts.title: How high should the fullbacks go?
strategyPrompts.body: Wide pressure can create chances, but it can also expose the channel. What balance should African teams strike?

strategyPrompts.title: Can set pieces carry a tight group?
strategyPrompts.body: When group matches are tense, corners and free kicks can decide everything. Which team has the best setup?

fanPolls.label: Fan poll
fanPolls.question: Which African team is best placed for the deepest run?
fanPolls.note: Poll buttons are an interface shell until backend persistence exists.

fanPolls.label: Fan poll
fanPolls.question: What matters most in the first African-team match?
fanPolls.note: No public vote totals are shown until a backend exists.

postMatchReactions.label: Post-match reaction
postMatchReactions.title: Three questions after the whistle
postMatchReactions.summary: After verified results arrive, use this slot for one tactical note, one player note, and one group-table implication.

resultMeaningTemplates.label: What this result means
resultMeaningTemplates.summary: A win changes the pressure map, but Matchday OS will only publish implications after final scores are verified.

resultMeaningTemplates.label: What this result means
resultMeaningTemplates.summary: A draw can be useful or dangerous depending on group context, goal difference, and the next fixture window.

tomorrowInAfricanFootball.label: Tomorrow in African football
tomorrowInAfricanFootball.title: Fixture watchlist and room prep
tomorrowInAfricanFootball.summary: Use tomorrow's slot for verified kickoff times, travel notes, watch-party setup, and one prediction question.

matchOfTheDay.label: Match of the day
matchOfTheDay.title: Morocco watch opener
matchOfTheDay.summary: Featured match slot is ready, but opponent, venue, and kickoff remain placeholders until verified.

playerToWatch.label: Player to watch
playerToWatch.title: Player slot pending verified squads
playerToWatch.summary: This slot will name a player only after verified squad and availability details are added.

groupPressureMeter.label: Group pressure meter
groupPressureMeter.summary: Pressure meter will move after verified group draw, schedule order, and final scores exist.

shareCardTemplates.label: Today's African matches
shareCardTemplates.description: A matchday desk card for verified African-team fixtures in the selected city time zone.

shareCardTemplates.label: My predictions today
shareCardTemplates.description: A local-device prediction summary card. Backend persistence is not implied.

shareCardTemplates.label: My leaderboard rank
shareCardTemplates.description: A leaderboard shell card that stays pending until verified scoring and backend rows exist.

shareCardTemplates.label: Country room support
shareCardTemplates.description: A team support card for sharing a Matchday OS country room.

shareCardTemplates.label: Viewing-center flyer
shareCardTemplates.description: A venue-friendly flyer for watch parties, kickoff prep, and WhatsApp invites.

shareCardTemplates.label: Group table snapshot
shareCardTemplates.description: A standings card that shows only static/provisional table data from the Matchday OS data layer.

shareCardTemplates.label: Knockout path
shareCardTemplates.description: A bracket-path shell using placeholder round labels until verified knockout teams are known.

shareCardTemplates.label: Match result reaction
shareCardTemplates.description: A post-match reaction template that avoids publishing unverified scores.

shareCardTemplates.label: Prize challenge invite
shareCardTemplates.description: A free-to-enter, skill-based challenge invitation with no betting or paid-entry framing.

shareCardTemplates.label: Sponsor-branded daily card
shareCardTemplates.description: A sponsor-safe daily card with a clearly labeled optional sponsor badge placeholder.

commercialInventory.audienceDataPolicy: Current analytics and audience notes are shared privately during sponsorship conversations. Public pages must not invent traffic, reach, sponsor logos, or audience numbers.
fanSafeRules: Sponsors cannot claim official tournament affiliation.
Sponsors cannot use official FIFA branding, trophy likenesses, or protected marks through Matchday OS.
Sponsor placements must not block core fixtures, teams, predictions, rooms, or share-card utility.
Sponsor assets must be lightweight and locally hosted or approved before launch.
Sponsor copy cannot alter standings, Prediction Points, Fan Points, fixture data, rules, cash-prize eligibility, winner verification, or editorial conclusions.

placements.label: Matchday OS main sponsor
placements.surface: Hero, sponsor one-pager, and clearly labeled utility-side placements

placements.label: Prediction challenge sponsor
placements.surface: Challenge page, prize rules page, and free-to-enter skill challenge CTAs for the $1,200 Top 10 verified-player prize pool

placements.label: Leaderboard sponsor
placements.surface: Top 20 public tracker shell and verified Prediction Points leaderboard once backend scoring exists. Only the final verified Top 10 receive cash prizes.

placements.label: African teams tracker sponsor
placements.surface: Team tracker support strip and room discovery modules

placements.label: Country room sponsor
placements.surface: Individual African team rooms with team-safe sponsor labeling

placements.label: Daily match card sponsor
placements.surface: Daily share-card and editorial desk card templates

placements.label: Viewing-center flyer sponsor
placements.surface: Viewing-center flyer, poster, and WhatsApp event card generator

placements.label: Newsletter/update sponsor
placements.surface: Matchday update signup and newsletter mentions

placements.label: Blog/strategy post sponsor
placements.surface: Clearly labeled sponsor-safe feature posts and strategy previews

buyerQuestions.question: Who can brands reach?
buyerQuestions.answer: African football fans, diaspora viewers, country-room followers, viewing-center operators, sports media buyers, sponsors, and brands looking for useful tournament-period utility. Current analytics are shared privately during sponsorship conversations.

buyerQuestions.question: What can be sponsored?
buyerQuestions.answer: Main hub placements, the free-to-enter $1,200 Prediction Challenge after backend and legal gates, Top 20 public tracker surfaces after verified scoring exists, team tracker strips, country rooms, daily cards, viewing-center flyers, newsletter updates, and sponsor-safe editorial features.

buyerQuestions.question: What does a pilot include?
buyerQuestions.answer: A scoped placement, sponsor-safe copy review, CTA routing, launch checklist, lightweight creative requirements, and a reporting window matched to available analytics.

buyerQuestions.question: How fast can launch happen?
buyerQuestions.answer: Simple labeled placements can be prepared quickly after sponsor copy, creative, target countries, CTA destination, legal restrictions, and reporting needs are confirmed. Prize and leaderboard sponsorships require backend locks, anti-cheat review, Top 10 verification, and rules gates first.

buyerQuestions.question: How do brands request current audience data?
buyerQuestions.answer: Use the Matchday OS sponsor enquiry route. AfroTools can share current analytics and audience notes privately so public pages do not invent traffic claims.

buyerQuestions.question: What sponsor influence is not allowed?
buyerQuestions.answer: Sponsors cannot change fixture data, standings, Prediction Points, Fan Points, cash-prize eligibility, winner verification, legal rules, editorial conclusions, or product trust copy.
